rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 2, 2018 Line 12, Plaintiff Jordan Medina'S Motion To Challenge Confidentiality Designation Of Documents Subject To Stipulated Non Sharing Protective Order * Redacted * Plaintiff Jordan Medina's motion to challenge confidentiality designation of documents subject to stipulated non-sharing protective order is granted. Documents labeled UBER 00086-00198 and 00208-00228 are no longer designated confidential per the parties' protective order. Except for the portions of the report concerning an award of fees and costs to Mr. Median, the court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead. Mr. Mead's recommendations concerning an award of fees and costs are not adopted because Mr. Medina did not seek either fees or costs in his moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHERYL LUNA VS. AVRAHAM SARANGA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 2, 2018 Line 7, CROSS COMPLAINANT AVRAHAM SARANGA's DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-complainant Avraham Saranga's demurrer to all 26 affirmative defenses in cross-defendant Cheryl Luna's answer to the cross-complaint is overruled in its entirety. While the filing of a demurrer to an answer is not sanctionable conduct as there is a basis for such a filing per FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 346, the filing of this demurrer is a complete waste of time and ruling upon it does nothing to further the litigation in this case. The affirmative defenses are alleged in a way that conforms to long-established and accepted practice in the Bay Area state courts. The purpose of this practice is to preserve a defendant's options until the defendant is able to learn more about the plaintiff's case. The proper and well-accepted procedure for a plaintiff who confronts affirmative defenses such as those alleged by Ms. Luna is, after a reasonable time has elapsed, to serve form interrogatory 15.1 and, if no information is provided as to particular affirmative defenses, seek a stipulation that those affirmative defenses are withdrawn or, failing that, file a motion for summary adjudication. Counsel for Ms. Luna is admonished that her filing of a sur-reply was not permitted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Luna must prepare the proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ARACELY RODRIGUEZ VS. DRYBAR HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 2, 2018 Line 11, DEFENDANT DRYBAR HOLDINGS LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Defendant Drybar Holdings LLC's demurrer to the all causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Aracely Rodriguez on grounds of another action pending is sustained without leave to amend and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of the Perera case pending in Los Angeles Superior Court or further order of this court. The claims alleged and the recovery sought in this case is identical to the claims alleged and recovery sought in Perera which was filed prior to the filing of this case. Per CCP 430.10(c) and the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction, this case must be stayed pending the conclusion of Perera. The fact that some of the claims alleged in this case were not included in Perera until after the filing of this case does not support or require a different result. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Drybar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
